subject: [PATCH 0/2] fuse: Fix possible memleak at startup with immediate teardown

reviews.author: Horst Birthelmer
reviews.summary: Reviewer Horst Birthelmer suggested removing a check in fuse_uring_abort() as it is unnecessary and complicates the code, while also implying that the patch's reliance on fuse_abort_conn() calling fuse_uring_abort() is sufficient to address the issue.
reviews.raw_body:
Ok so you rely on the fact that fuse_abort_conn() will call 
fuse_uring_abort() and that sets queue->stopped.
This could work, but I would still remove the check for
queue_refs > 0 in fuse_uring_abort(), since it just complicates things
for no real reason.

2026-04-11.patch_summary: This patch addresses a potential memory leak and hang issue in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module. The problem arises from concurrent access to the queue references, leading to ent entries being leaked or causing the system to wait indefinitely for queue references to drop to zero. The patch ensures that the async teardown worker is running, guaranteeing that the ent entry will be properly torn down and preventing memory leaks and hangs.

2026-04-10.patch_summary: This patch addresses a potential memory leak and hang issue in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module. The problem arises from concurrent access to the queue references, leading to ent entries being leaked or causing the system to wait indefinitely for queue references to drop to zero. The patch ensures that the async teardown worker is running, guaranteeing that the ent entry will be properly torn down and preventing memory leaks and hangs.
